heroHeadline: Accessibility Crisis Emerges as Costs Rise for 2026 World Cup
heroSubtitle: The Bay Area has secured nearly $60 million in federal funding for World Cup hosting at Levi's Stadium, covering security, transportation, and infrastructure. Meanwhile, Mexico vs South Africa has been confirmed as the Group A opener with fixture scheduling now public two months before the tournament begins.
footerNote: Data sourced from FIFA.com, UEFA.com, US Soccer, CONCACAF, ESPN, The Athletic, BBC Sport, AP, Reuters, NPR, Al Jazeera, Human Rights Watch, Amnesty International, American Immigration Council, Middle East Monitor, CBS Sports, Bloomberg, Fortune, UPI, CNN, Inside World Football
